key_facts: Verduzco Ruiz is a citizen and national of Mexico
He was deemed inadmissible in 2000, ordered removed, and was in fact removed
In 2017, his wife filed a second I-130 on his behalf, which was approved
He applied for adjustment of status and was investigated for false testimony
DHS reinstated the removal order in May 2018
Verduzco Ruiz was advised of his rights and declined to make a statement or sign the Acknowledgement and Response
legal_issues: Impermissibly retroactive application of IIRIRA's reinstatement provision
Denial of right to counsel in reinstatement proceedings
decision: The panel denied Alfonso Verduzco Ruiz’s petition for review of an order of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) reinstating his prior order of removal
reasoning_summary: The panel held that it was not impermissibly retroactive to apply IIRIRA's expanded reinstatement provision to Verduzco Ruiz because he took no affirmative step to adjust his status before IIRIRA went into effect. The panel also rejected Verduzco Ruiz's argument that his due process right to counsel was violated when DHS did not invite his counsel to attend reinstatement proceedings, citing that an alien must show prejudice to obtain relief for a due process violation based on denial of any right to counsel that may exist in reinstatement proceedings
